message:
fix(channel): replace wall-clock timestamps with deterministic evidence event ordering

Channel simulation evidence entries (clock_skew, crash, fault, partition)
previously used SystemTime::now() for ts_unix_ms, making evidence logs
non-deterministic and unreproducible across replays.

Clock skew: evidence timestamps now derive from the base clock's Time value
converted to milliseconds, ensuring the evidence timeline matches the
simulated clock domain rather than the host wall clock.

Crash controller: introduces an evidence_seq AtomicU64 counter that
produces monotonically increasing timestamps (1, 2, 3, ...) for crash,
restart, restart_exhausted, and send_rejected events. This makes crash
evidence fully deterministic regardless of host timing.

Fault and partition controllers: same pattern — evidence_seq counter
replaces SystemTime::now() for all evidence emissions.

All four modules update emit_*_evidence() to accept an explicit ts_unix_ms
parameter instead of computing it internally.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
